ead.archdesc:
======= RMM02531Romeyn Berry family papers2531Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections

rghisThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:29:29 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Romeyn Berry family papers
Berry, Romeyn, 1881-1957.
Gould, Hannah Wright, 1819-1912.
2531
11.5 cubic feet. 11.5 cubic feet.
1636-1960. Correspondence. Correspondence includes personal and business correspondence; Berry's letters from Kenneth Roberts, E.B. White, and Allison Danzig; one letter from Major William B. Miles concerning the activities of the Seventh Regiment, New York National Guard; correspondence of Berry's second wife, Hester Bancroft Berry, her father, Wilder D. Bancroft, and other members of the Bancroft family. Writings include copy and clippings from his "Talk of the Town" columns in The New Yorker; articles from the Ithaca Journal and Cornell Alumni News; radio broadcasts; and books and pamphlets by others. Diaries and journals by Berry, Katherine Bott Bancroft, and Hester Bancroft Berry, 1943-1951; farm accounts from "Stoneposts," Jacksonville, N.Y., 1936-1938; drawings; family and other photographs; legal documents and related items referring to the Berry and Gould families; newspaper clippings relating to Professor Wilder D. Bancroft; items having Ithaca or local interest, including Lyceum Theater accounts, 1893-1923; and miscellaneous family items. Also, student scrapbook of Olive Nutting Berry at Wells College, with material about social events at Cornell University. Also, correspondence between Romeyn Berry, his grandmother Hannah Wright Gould, his mother, and others, mostly when he was an undergraduate at Cornell; condolence letters after Berry's death, written to his widow Hester Bancroft Berry, to his daughter, Hilda Berry Sanford, and to Ruth Berry McDonald; program from the memorial stone dedication in 1988; magazine and newspaper articles; trust agreement; and typescript version of Hannah Wright Gould's 1871"Garden Journal," with a pen-and-ink drawing on wood of Romeyn and Harriet Berry as children. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 1 2 3 4 6 7 8 9 ======= 1 1 2 3 4 6 7 8 9 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2085762
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
October 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, October 2018
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Romeyn Berry (pronounced Ro-Mine) graduated from Cornell University in 1904. His nickname was Rym, pronounced rim. Columnist, author, lawyer, farmer. He served as graduate Director of Cornell Athletics from 1919 to 1935.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence includes personal and business correspondence; Berry's letters from Kenneth Roberts, E.B. White, and Allison Danzig; one letter from Major William B. Miles concerning the activities of the Seventh Regiment, New York National Guard; correspondence of Berry's second wife, Hester Bancroft Berry, her father, Wilder D. Bancroft, and other members of the Bancroft family. Writings include copy and clippings from his "Talk of the Town" columns in The New Yorker; articles from the Ithaca Journal and Cornell Alumni News; radio broadcasts; and books and pamphlets by others. Diaries and journals by Berry, Katherine Bott Bancroft, and Hester Bancroft Berry, 1943-1951; farm accounts from "Stoneposts," Jacksonville, N.Y., 1936-1938; drawings; family and other photographs; legal documents and related items referring to the Berry and Gould families; newspaper clippings relating to Professor Wilder D. Bancroft; items having Ithaca or local interest, including Lyceum Theater accounts, 1893-1923; and miscellaneous family items. Also, student scrapbook of Olive Nutting Berry at Wells College, with material about social events at Cornell University.
Also, correspondence between Romeyn Berry, his grandmother Hannah Wright Gould, his mother, and others, mostly when he was an undergraduate at Cornell; condolence letters after Berry's death, written to his widow Hester Bancroft Berry, to his daughter, Hilda Berry Sanford, and to Ruth Berry McDonald; program from the memorial stone dedication in 1988; magazine and newspaper articles; trust agreement; and typescript version of Hannah Wright Gould's 1871"Garden Journal," with a pen-and-ink drawing on wood of Romeyn and Harriet Berry as children.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Romeyn Berry
Legal Papers Athletic Documents Correspondence Publications, Speeches, Broadcasts, and Plays War Materials Personal Material and General Documents Cornell Material
Series II. Harriet V.V. Berry and Elizabeth Berry
Series III. Berry/Gould Family Material
Series IV. Hester Bancroft Berry and Wilder Bancroft
Series V. Olive Nutting Berry and Nutting Family
Series VI. Photographs and artwork
Series VII. Medals, Ribbons, and objects
Cite As:
Romeyn Berry family papers, #2531. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Diaries. Drawings. Scrapbooks. Photographs. Account books. Gardening Authors. Lawyers. Journalists. Women college students. Families -- New York (State) Farms -- New York (State) -- Jacksonville. New Yorker Berry, Hester Bancroft, 1899-1963. Bancroft, Wilder D. (Wilder Dwight), 1867-1953. Bancroft, Katherine Bott. Berry, Olive Nutting. Gould, Hannah Wright, 1819-1912. Sanford, Hilda Truslow Berry, 1916- McDonald, Ruth Seely Berry, 1913- Danzig, Allison. Roberts, Kenneth Lewis, 1885-1957. White, E. B. (Elwyn Brooks), 1899-1985. Bancroft family. Berry family. Cornell University -- : Students. Wells College -- : Students. Lyceum Theater (Ithaca, N.Y.) Cornell University. Class of 1904.

Scope and Contents
Includes some invitations, programs, and publications like "The Winged Foot". Also includes handwritten loose speeches and articles. The back of the scrapbook has clippings on the Progressive Party, Berry's certification as attorney (1914), and a photograph of a Progressive Party banner with Berry running for assembly.Cornell/Princeton statistics and score sheetUndated348Subseries Ic. Scope and Contents
Scrapbook includes: photographs of people and athletic events (baseball, boating, and football), clippings, invitations and programs, ticket stubs, football programs, Beta Theta Pi material, Athletic Association documents and programs, trade stakes, "The Widow" documents and programs, track documents, ribbons and awards, 1903 red beanie, and a Macaronigram.Lyceum Theater accounts1893-1923923Series II. Includes The Architectural Record, The Lantern Westover School Magazine, Brookgreen Gardens History, A Century of Growth, and more5 pp. of biographical data concerning Warner ("Butch") Berry's first three years of lifeUndated711"History of the Village of Brookton" (dictated by Cantine Lounsbery, July 11, 1907, 2 pp. typescript)1907-07-11711U.S. Geological Survey Map of New York - Ithaca Quadrangle1929711Berry Family TreeUndated711Hand drawn calling cards/place settingsUndated711Rutgers College commencement program1974-07-17711Various documentsUndated711

Includes hand drawn map from Ithaca to Pittsfield,Daily journal, writer unknown1865711A small notebook partially filled with notes on animal diseases and notations "Stock Book" (owner unknown).1939627Volumes III and V of daily farm diary, possibly written by Romeyn Berry1943-195010Ruth Berry Sunday school handoutsUndated98Hannah Gould Garden Diary193099

Scope and Contents
Romeyn or Hester Berry'sMounted image of saintUndated928Indenture - Yorkshire16361Indenture - Lancashire, England16911Series IV. Hester Bancroft Berry and Wilder BancroftBancroft family correspondence1881-1941628
Scope and Contents
Letters to George and John C. Bancroft (the letter to George appears to be from John, but the last page is missing) of May 11 and 12, 1881, concerning the will of Abisha Larned of Oxford, Mass., a copy of the will, and other relevant information; a letter from Peter Cantine (location?) to Charles F. Mulks (Ithaca), Jan. 15, 1895, re: Cantine's family genealogy, which Mulks had apparently requested, and in turn requesting information from Mulks in order to "prove" the will of a Samuel W. Ransom; two letters to Wilder D. Bancroft from Mark A. DeWolfe Howe (July 15 and Dec. 14, 1904); fifteen letters (Nov. 20-23, 1906) from various British scientists to Sir William Ramsay, regarding Wilder Bancroft's suggestion that the Journal of Physical Chemistry be published in England; letters to Hester Bancroft in Europe (ca. 1926), including a letter from Morris Bishop (Feb. 18, 1926); and other letters to Hester and Romeyn Berry, pertaining to Hester Berry's sculptures, the launching of the U.S.S. Bancroft, publication of Romeyn's work by Putnam's, and other topics. Also a printed Christmas card from Sir Wilfred and Lady Grenfell (undated.) and a Christmas card from a drawing by Hester Berry (May 11, 1881-Mar. 31, 1953).Wilder Bancroft will1939-12-04712Inventory of Wilder Bancroft estate items1953712
Scope and Contents
Art objects displayed and stored at the Worcester Art Museum, Worcester, Mass. Acc. Nos. given by each item.Newspaper clippings1923-1955713
Scope and Contents
Clippings (25 items) from many newspapers relating to Professor Wilder Bancroft's experiments in rain-making (1923); a page from the Ithaca Journal about early explorers and travellers in Tompkins County (Jan. 26, 1955); and other items."Stoneposts - Stock on Hand," a book kept by Hester Berry - egg, cream sales, etc.1936-1938629A European travel diary kept by Katherine Bott (later Mrs. Wilder Bancroft) in the summer1880630"Greenhouse Diary," a small partially filled notebook kept by Hester Berry1934630Hester Bancroft passport, receipts1899631Mrs. Romeyn Berry name plateUndated631Correspondence pertaining to family estates1952-1954632Poems by Hester Bancroft1919633Poems by Hester Bancroft1914-1916634A notebook of poems written about or during World War I compiled by Hester Bancroft.1914-1919635Series V. Olive Nutting Berry and Nutting FamilyLee Nutting civil war record and tax documents1908-1927636Olive Berry indenture1919-08-18714Olive Nutting Berry scrapbook1902-19128
Scope and Contents
Includes invitations to social functions at Cornell University and elsewhere, programs, postcards, photographs; Wells College material including a manual, constitution and by-laws and two copies of the Wells College Chronicle, correspondence, poetry from Romeyn Berry to Olive, formal announcement of the their wedding, calling cards, a copy of the Cornell Alumni News and photographs of fraternity parties at Cornell.Printed items removed from Olive Nutting Berry scrapbook1903-190981Olive Nutting Berry scrapbook items, loose1902-191282-4Olive Lee Berry memorial from Garden Club of IthacaUndated85Olive Lee Berry card and stationary plate receiptsUndated85Article from the Ithaca Daily News on Cornell Crew, with R.W. Nutting1904-07-2991Series VI. Scope and Contents
Romeyn or Hester Berry'sMounted image of saintUndated928Indenture - Yorkshire16361Indenture - Lancashire, England16911Series IV. Hester Bancroft Berry and Wilder BancroftBancroft family correspondence1881-1941628
Scope and Contents
Letters to George and John C. Bancroft (the letter to George appears to be from John, but the last page is missing) of May 11 and 12, 1881, concerning the will of Abisha Larned of Oxford, Mass., a copy of the will, and other relevant information; a letter from Peter Cantine (location?) to Charles F. Mulks (Ithaca), Jan. 15, 1895, re: Cantine's family genealogy, which Mulks had apparently requested, and in turn requesting information from Mulks in order to "prove" the will of a Samuel W. Ransom; two letters to Wilder D. Bancroft from Mark A. DeWolfe Howe (July 15 and Dec. 14, 1904); fifteen letters (Nov. 20-23, 1906) from various British scientists to Sir William Ramsay, regarding Wilder Bancroft's suggestion that the Journal of Physical Chemistry be published in England; letters to Hester Bancroft in Europe (ca. 1926), including a letter from Morris Bishop (Feb. 18, 1926); and other letters to Hester and Romeyn Berry, pertaining to Hester Berry's sculptures, the launching of the U.S.S. Bancroft, publication of Romeyn's work by Putnam's, and other topics. Also a printed Christmas card from Sir Wilfred and Lady Grenfell (undated.) and a Christmas card from a drawing by Hester Berry (May 11, 1881-Mar. 31, 1953).Wilder Bancroft will1939-12-04712Inventory of Wilder Bancroft estate items1953712
Scope and Contents
Art objects displayed and stored at the Worcester Art Museum, Worcester, Mass. Acc. Nos. given by each item.Newspaper clippings1923-1955713
Scope and Contents
Clippings (25 items) from many newspapers relating to Professor Wilder Bancroft's experiments in rain-making (1923); a page from the Ithaca Journal about early explorers and travellers in Tompkins County (Jan. 26, 1955); and other items."Stoneposts - Stock on Hand," a book kept by Hester Berry - egg, cream sales, etc.1936-1938629A European travel diary kept by Katherine Bott (later Mrs. Wilder Bancroft) in the summer1880630"Greenhouse Diary," a small partially filled notebook kept by Hester Berry1934630Hester Bancroft passport, receipts1899631Mrs. Romeyn Berry name plateUndated631Correspondence pertaining to family estates1952-1954632Poems by Hester Bancroft1919633Poems by Hester Bancroft1914-1916634A notebook of poems written about or during World War I compiled by Hester Bancroft.1914-1919635Series V. Olive Nutting Berry and Nutting FamilyLee Nutting civil war record and tax documents1908-1927636Olive Berry indenture1919-08-18714Olive Nutting Berry scrapbook1902-19128
Scope and Contents
Includes invitations to social functions at Cornell University and elsewhere, programs, postcards, photographs; Wells College material including a manual, constitution and by-laws and two copies of the Wells College Chronicle, correspondence, poetry from Romeyn Berry to Olive, formal announcement of the their wedding, calling cards, a copy of the Cornell Alumni News and photographs of fraternity parties at Cornell.Printed items removed from Olive Nutting Berry scrapbook1903-190981Olive Nutting Berry scrapbook items, loose1902-191282-4Olive Lee Berry memorial from Garden Club of IthacaUndated85Olive Lee Berry card and stationary plate receiptsUndated85Article from the Ithaca Daily News on Cornell Crew, with R.W. Nutting1904-07-2991Series VI.
